title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for dead lettering, poison messages, delivery limits, and parking and requires the smallest defensible dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz messaging team handles case 6548 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
